assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explains increased preemption risk during upgrades and mentions cascading preemptions, addressing interaction with drain process

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response doesn't specifically mention that workloads on spot instances are typically designed for interruption, which would make upgrades easier

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends conservative maxSurge of 1-2 nodes, not higher maxSurge for spot pools

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends maxUnavailable: 0 to prevent service disruption, opposite of the expected advice

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions spot capacity constraints, availability issues, and that new nodes might not be available in preferred instance types

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudget configuration example and recommends their use

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl patch command, not gcloud command for configuring surge settings

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests upgrading on-demand instances first, then spot instances, which is opposite of the expected recommendation
